pattern: Membership in a small fixed alphabet is a constant-time question once the alphabet is stored as a set, so checking a whole word reduces to checking each of its characters against that set.
hints: You only ever need to know whether a character belongs to the allowed set, never its position, so a set (or a 26-bit mask) is enough.
A word is consistent exactly when every character it contains is in that allowed set; one disallowed character disqualifies the whole word.
Build the set once from `allowed`, then for each word check all of its characters against it, counting the words where every character passes.

edge_inputs.description: a small allowed set with some words passing and some failing
every word consists of exactly one allowed letter
allowed is the full alphabet, so every word is consistent
words that repeat allowed letters many times are still consistent
no word is consistent at all

stress.description: many random lowercase words checked against a random allowed subset
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    import string
    letters = list(string.ascii_lowercase)
    rng.shuffle(letters)
    k = rng.randint(1, 26)
    allowed = ''.join(letters[:k])
    n = max(1, min(size, 10000))
    words = []
    for _ in range(n):
        wlen = rng.randint(1, 10)
        words.append(''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(wlen)))
    return [allowed, words]
